stages.title: Site Analysis
stages.narration: Stage 1. Site analysis. A 6 by 8 meter resilient interlocking brick house is laid out on a structural grid with verified bearing capacity and drainage.
stages.explanation: Construction begins with site selection and layout. Proper orientation, drainage and bearing capacity are verified before excavation.

stages.title: Setting Out
stages.narration: Stage 2. Setting out. Grid lines at 1 meter modules establish wall centerlines and foundation trenches aligned to the structural grid.
stages.explanation: Setting out transfers design dimensions from drawings to the field with right-angle verification.

stages.title: Excavation
stages.narration: Stage 3. Excavation. Trenches are cut to 1 meter depth to competent bearing stratum for strip footings on all four sides.
stages.explanation: Excavation reaches competent bearing strata capable of supporting structural loads from the 6×8 m envelope.

stages.title: PCC Blinding
stages.narration: Stage 4. Plain cement concrete blinding is placed in trenches to create a level working platform.
stages.explanation: 75 mm PCC creates a clean, level bearing plane while preventing contamination of structural concrete.

stages.title: Footings & Foundation Wall
stages.narration: Stage 5. Strip footings and foundation wall masonry transfer building loads to bearing soil.
stages.explanation: 600 mm wide strip footings with 300 mm depth spread wall loads; foundation wall rises to plinth level.

stages.title: Plinth Beam & DPC
stages.narration: Stage 6. Plinth beam formwork, reinforcement cage and concrete tie the foundation. DPC layer prevents moisture rise.
stages.explanation: 450 mm plinth height with 230 mm RC plinth beam ties the structure and reduces differential settlement.

stages.title: Vertical Reinforcement
stages.narration: Stage 7. Twelve millimeter vertical bars at corners, mid-walls and openings are anchored into the plinth beam.
stages.explanation: Vertical reinforcement improves seismic performance, connects to lintel and roof bands, and prevents wall separation.

stages.title: Interlocking Brick Walls
stages.narration: Stage 8. Interlocking brick courses are laid course-by-course with corner interlocking. Each block snaps into place with no floating or overlapping units.
stages.explanation: 230 mm thick interlocking masonry with staggered bond improves load transfer and reduces mortar dependence.

stages.title: Lintel Band
stages.narration: Stage 9. Reinforced concrete lintel band — formwork, rebar cage, concrete pour and completed band at opening level.
stages.explanation: 150 mm lintel band provides horizontal continuity at 2.1 m, preventing wall separation during seismic loading.

stages.title: Roof Band
stages.narration: Stage 10. Seismic roof band with rebar, formwork and concrete creates box action at wall top.
stages.explanation: Roof band at 3 m wall height connects vertical bars and enables box action for earthquake load sharing.

stages.title: Roof Structure
stages.narration: Stage 11. Timber trusses, purlins, bracing and connections are assembled on the wall plate. 15 degree roof slope.
stages.explanation: Trusses at 2 m spacing with purlins and bracing form a lightweight diaphragm transferring loads to walls.

stages.title: Roof Covering & Complete
stages.narration: Stage 12. Roof sheets are placed panel-by-panel. Construction complete — resilient 6×8 m interlocking brick house ready for occupancy.
stages.explanation: Metal roof sheets complete the diaphragm. Full load path: roof → walls → plinth → footings → soil.

foundation.title: Foundation System
foundation.purpose: Transfers vertical loads from walls and roof to bearing soil.
foundation.loadMechanism: Strip footing spreads pressure; PCC provides uniform bearing plane.

footing.title: Strip Footing
footing.purpose: Spreads wall loads over a wider bearing area in competent soil.
footing.loadMechanism: Concrete strip under all load-bearing walls; 300 mm depth minimum.

plinth_beam.title: Plinth Beam
plinth_beam.purpose: Ties foundation elements and supports wall loads at 450 mm plinth height.
plinth_beam.reinforcement: 4–6 bars 12–16 mm with stirrups @ 150 mm c/c
plinth_beam.function: Reduces cracking, aligns wall base, anchors vertical bars

dpc.title: Damp Proof Course
dpc.purpose: Prevents capillary rise of moisture into wall masonry.
dpc.loadMechanism: Continuous membrane at plinth level; no structural load.

wall.title: Interlocking Brick Wall
wall.purpose: Primary load-bearing envelope — 230 mm thick, 3 m height.
wall.compression: Bearing stress distributed through interlock keys
wall.jointMechanism: Dry-stack keys reduce sliding between courses
wall.loadTransfer: Compression to lintel band and roof band

vertical_reinforcement.title: Vertical Reinforcement
vertical_reinforcement.purpose: Provides ductility and holds walls together under earthquake.
vertical_reinforcement.anchorage: L-bar into plinth beam with 480 mm development length
vertical_reinforcement.seismicRole: Connects footing to lintel and roof bands; prevents corner separation

lintel_band.title: Lintel Band
lintel_band.purpose: Horizontal tie at opening level resisting wall separation during seismic shaking.
lintel_band.earthquakeMechanism: Continuous 150 mm RC ring beam at 2.1 m keeps walls acting as a box

roof_band.title: Roof Band
roof_band.purpose: Seismic tie at wall top creating box action with lintel band.
roof_band.boxAction: Walls cannot move independently; inertia forces shared through diaphragm

roof_structure.title: Roof Structure
roof_structure.purpose: Timber trusses, purlins and bracing at 15° slope.
roof_structure.loadMechanism: Trusses span 6 m width; purlins transfer sheet loads to trusses

roof_truss.title: Roof Truss
roof_truss.purpose: Primary roof framing element spanning building width.
roof_truss.loadMechanism: Bottom chord in tension; rafters carry purlin and sheet loads

roof_cover.title: Roof Covering
roof_cover.purpose: Metal sheets completing the roof diaphragm.
roof_cover.loadMechanism: Sheets span between purlins; wind uplift resisted by fixings
